betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Precision Laser Etcher§f is a 3×4×4 multiblock (TilePrecisionLaserEtcher.java) that uses the §6Vacuum Laser Emitter§f (10014) to etch §6Circuit Plates§f into §6Integrated Circuits§f — the core ingredient for §6Control Circuit Boards§f, §6Item IO Boards§f, and every other AR logic part.

§6Production Path (libVulpes + TilePrecisionLaserEtcher.java)§f:
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: 1x §6Gold Plate§f (libvulpes:productplate:2), 1x §6Item IO Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:4), 1x §6Machine Structure§f (libvulpes:structuremachine), 1x §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3), 1x §6Advanced Circuit§f (advancedrocketry:ic:2)
- Adjacent component: §6Vacuum Laser Emitter§f (10014) must be placed and active
- Power: 60+ RF/t depending on recipe tier
- JEI category: §6Advanced Rocketry: Precision Laser Etcher§f

The etcher's main role in the AR progression is producing the high-density §6Integrated Circuit Plate§f and §6Advanced Circuit Board§f used to build the §6Chemical Reactor§f (10017), §6Electrolyser§f (10016), §6Centrifuge§f (10018), and ultimately the §6Atmosphere Analyzer§f (10062) and §6Orbital Laser Drill§f. It also etches the precision masks for satellite components.

§eTip§f: The etcher's structure is symmetrical — you can mirror the assembly in any cardinal direction. Power it through a §6Power Plug§f on the bottom layer; the top is a §6Control Circuit Board§f facing the controller block.
